title: schradera brasiliensis
intro: Schradera brasiliensis, also known as Strychnine tree or Quina, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to expel parasites and aid in the treatment of fever and malaria. The health benefits of Schradera brasiliensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to prevent the spread of certain diseases. Herbal preparations of Schradera brasiliensis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, although caution should be taken due to its toxic properties when ingested in large quantities.
